raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to delete files matching patterns under that directory tree. Use find. Patterns: .DS_Store, '._.DS_Store', '._*', '.TemporaryItems', '.apdisk'. So find /system/folder3/temp -type f -name '.DS_Store' -o -name '._.DS_Store' -o -name '._*' -o -name '.TemporaryItems' -o -name '.apdisk' -delete. Also directories? It says files and directories under that tree that match with one of the name patterns. Some patterns may be directories? .TemporaryItems could be a directory. So use -type f -o -type d. So find ... \( -name ... -o -name ... \) -delete. Use -mindepth 1? But root itself not matched. So command: find /system/folder3/temp -depth \( -name '.DS_Store' -o -name '._.DS_Store' -o -name '._*' -o -name '.TemporaryItems' -o -name '.apdisk' \) -delete. Use -depth to delete directories after contents. Output single line.
